div1.entryFree: Behoove or Behove, vb., 1) to be advantageous to: if you know aught which does b. my knowledge thereof to be informed, Wint. I, 2, 395. there are cozeners abroad; therefore it --s men to be wary, IV, 4, 257. -- 2) to become: while these do labour for their own preferment, --s it us to labour for the realm, H6B I, 1, 182. you do not understand yourself so clearly as it --s my daughter and your honour, Hml. I, 3, 97. which --s me keep at utterance, Cymb. III, 1, 73. In Tim. III, 5, 22: with such sober and unnoted passion he did b. his anger, ere 'twas spent, as if he had but proved an argument, M. Edd. have changed the word to behave, but cf. to become Lr. IV, 3, 26 and Ant. I, 3, 84.
